governing_thesis: AccountCcy.com is the chain of custody for monetary truth inside enterprise finance.

pages.title: AccountCcy.com — The Chain of Custody for Monetary Truth
pages.meta_description: AccountCcy.com is the reference layer for account currency control inside enterprise finance. The chain of custody through which monetary movement becomes accounting truth.
pages.seo_intent: brand authority, category definition, framework positioning

pages.title: The Chain of Custody for Monetary Truth — AccountCcy.com
pages.meta_description: Money does not become accounting truth automatically. It becomes truth through custody. AccountCcy.com maps the chain through which monetary movement becomes defensible accounting reality.
pages.seo_intent: doctrine positioning, philosophical anchor, framework authority

pages.title: The Currency State Control Framework — AccountCcy.com
pages.meta_description: The Currency State Control Framework defines account currency as a financial control discipline — the governed passage through which monetary movement becomes accounting truth inside enterprise finance.
pages.seo_intent: framework authority, original methodology, category positioning

pages.title: The CCY State Chain — From Quoted Price to Audit Reality
pages.meta_description: The CCY State Chain maps eight currency states inside enterprise finance — from quoted price to audit reality. Account Currency is the critical control point at State 04.
pages.seo_intent: framework authority, definition of original doctrine, state-by-state reference

pages.title: What Is Account Currency? — Definition and Control Discipline
pages.meta_description: Account currency is the currency assigned to an account for recording, reconciling, and controlling financial balances inside an enterprise finance system. A reference definition.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — primary SEO anchor for account currency

pages.title: Transaction Currency vs Account Currency vs Reporting Currency
pages.meta_description: The same financial event can carry three distinct currency identities. Understanding the difference between transaction, account, and reporting currency is critical to multi-currency accounting accuracy.
pages.seo_intent: comparison intent — high-value professional SEO

pages.title: Account Currency in ERP Systems — Reference Architecture
pages.meta_description: Account currency configuration inside ERP systems governs how balances are maintained, revalued, reconciled, and reported. A reference architecture for enterprise finance practitioners.
pages.seo_intent: ERP application intent — practitioners searching for system-specific currency behavior

pages.title: Functional Currency Explained — Definition, Determination, and Control
pages.meta_description: Functional currency is the currency of the primary economic environment in which an entity operates. Its relationship to account currency, ledger currency, and reporting currency is critical to multi-currency accounting.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — functional currency, IFRS IAS 21, accounting standards

pages.title: Foreign Currency Revaluation and Translation — AccountCcy.com
pages.meta_description: Foreign currency revaluation and translation are the mechanisms through which account currency balances are converted into ledger, reporting, and consolidation currencies. Understanding the difference is critical.
pages.seo_intent: FX risk intent — practitioners searching for revaluation, translation, remeasurement

pages.title: CFO Guide to Hidden Account Currency Risk — AccountCcy.com
pages.meta_description: CFOs who manage FX exposure at the treasury level may still carry hidden account currency risk inside their ERP configuration. A governance guide for enterprise finance leaders.
pages.seo_intent: strategic intent — CFO, group controller, multi-currency accounting governance

pages.title: Account Currency Glossary — Enterprise Finance Reference
pages.meta_description: A precise reference glossary for account currency, multi-currency accounting, and enterprise finance terminology — organized by CCY State Chain position.
pages.seo_intent: glossary authority, topical anchor, long-tail SEO

pages.title: Currency State Diagnostic — Account Currency Control Maturity
pages.meta_description: Assess your organization's account currency control maturity across the CCY State Chain — from Quoted Currency to Audit Reality.
pages.seo_intent: tool intent, diagnostic, account currency maturity assessment

pages.title: Strategic Acquisition — AccountCcy.com
pages.meta_description: AccountCcy.com is a sovereign-grade reference asset for enterprise finance. Strategic acquisition inquiries are considered from aligned institutional organizations only.
pages.seo_intent: acquisition positioning — not public domain sale

pages.title: Quoted Currency — State 01 of the CCY State Chain
pages.meta_description: Quoted currency is the currency in which a price is expressed in a market. State 01 of the CCY State Chain — the pre-transaction rate before any accounting obligation is created.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — quoted currency, market rate, FX rate

pages.title: Transaction Currency — State 02 of the CCY State Chain
pages.meta_description: Transaction currency is the currency in which a financial event is denominated. State 02 of the CCY State Chain — where obligation is created and accounting begins.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — transaction currency accounting

pages.title: Settlement Currency — State 03 of the CCY State Chain
pages.meta_description: Settlement currency is the currency in which cash actually transfers between parties. State 03 of the CCY State Chain — divergence from transaction currency creates FX consequences.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — settlement currency, FX settlement

pages.title: Account Currency — State 04 — The Critical Control Point
pages.meta_description: Account currency is the currency assigned to an account inside an enterprise finance system. State 04 of the CCY State Chain — the control point where external movement enters accounting custody.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — account currency, control point, ERP account currency

pages.title: Ledger Currency — State 05 of the CCY State Chain
pages.meta_description: Ledger currency is the currency in which an entity's general ledger is maintained. State 05 of the CCY State Chain — typically aligned with functional currency under accounting standards.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — ledger currency, functional currency, general ledger

pages.title: Reporting Currency — State 06 of the CCY State Chain
pages.meta_description: Reporting currency is the currency in which financial results are presented for management, regulatory, or investor purposes. State 06 — translation gains and losses emerge here.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — reporting currency, presentation currency, translation

pages.title: Consolidation Currency — State 07 of the CCY State Chain
pages.meta_description: Consolidation currency is the group-level currency into which multiple entity ledgers are unified. State 07 of the CCY State Chain — maximum FX translation complexity.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — consolidation currency, group currency, multi-entity accounting

pages.title: Audit Reality — State 08 — The Terminal State of the CCY State Chain
pages.meta_description: Audit reality is the terminal state of the CCY State Chain — the condition in which monetary figures are traceable, supported, reconcilable, and defensible under professional scrutiny.
pages.seo_intent: definition intent — audit reality, audit trail, defensible accounting truth

pages.title: Account Currency Misconfiguration — Risk and Control
pages.meta_description: Account currency misconfiguration inside ERP systems can produce downstream distortion across ledger, reporting, consolidation, and audit layers. Understanding and controlling this risk is critical.
pages.seo_intent: risk intent — account currency risk, ERP misconfiguration

pages.title: Transaction Currency vs Account Currency — What Is the Difference?
pages.meta_description: Transaction currency and account currency are distinct identities for the same financial event. Confusing them creates reconciliation complexity and FX distortion in enterprise accounting.
pages.seo_intent: comparison intent — transaction currency vs account currency

pages.title: Account Currency vs Reporting Currency — Key Differences
pages.meta_description: Account currency and reporting currency are not the same. Account currency controls how balances are maintained. Reporting currency controls how results are presented. The difference matters to every controller.
pages.seo_intent: comparison intent — account currency vs reporting currency

pages.title: Currency State Diagnostic — CCY State Chain Maturity Assessment
pages.meta_description: Assess your organization's account currency control maturity across all eight states of the CCY State Chain. An interactive diagnostic framework from AccountCcy.com.
pages.seo_intent: tool intent — currency diagnostic, account currency maturity, ERP currency assessment

pages.title: Account Currency Control Checklist — AccountCcy.com
pages.meta_description: A structured checklist for reviewing account currency governance across ERP configuration, period-end controls, and audit readiness.
pages.seo_intent: tool intent — account currency checklist, ERP currency controls

publication_rules.sitemap_condition: status == published AND indexable == true AND quality_gate passed
publication_rules.link_rule: no page may link to a page with status != published
publication_rules.placeholder_rule: no placeholder content, no coming soon, no lorem ipsum
